title: add-two-cluster-verification
summary: Added `scripts/verify-two-cluster.sh`, a 7-section end-to-end verification script proving the two-cluster VSO demo works (contexts, Vault/VSO placement in each cluster, network reachability, real Kubernetes-auth login, VaultStaticSecret sync, and rotation), wired it up as `make verify-two-cluster`, and added a unit test suite; live-verified against the real `kind-vault-lab`/`kind-vso-lab` clusters including deliberate failure injection.
keyLearnings: - `kubectl create token <sa> --duration Xm` requires a minimum of 10 minutes (`may not specify a duration less than 10 minutes`) — used `10m` for the vso-demo SA JWT minted for the live auth/kubernetes-vso login check.

- Section 5 (kubernetes auth) does a *real* `vault write auth/kubernetes-vso/login role=vso-demo jwt=<minted-jwt>` and checks for `.auth.client_token` via `jq` — this proves actual auth works, not just that the mount exists.

- Section 6 compares `vault kv get -field=username kv-v2/vault-demo/mysecret` (Vault cluster) directly against the native Secret's base64-decoded value (VSO cluster) rather than hardcoding an expected string, so it's robust to whatever the current baseline is.

- Section 7 (rotation) always attempts to reset `kv-v2/vault-demo/mysecret` back to `BASELINE_USERNAME` even on the failure path (before calling `fail_section`), so a failed run never leaves the demo secret rotated; live-verified full round trip took ~6 poll attempts (~18s) with default `ROTATION_SLEEP=3`.

- Negative-placement checks (Vault absent from VSO cluster; VSO operator/CRDs/`vso-demo` namespace absent from Vault cluster) are asserted directly, matching the exit-criteria's "negative placement" requirement.
- `--check-only` only validates required commands + `require_contexts`, never touches a live cluster beyond that — matches the pattern used by every other script in `scripts/`.
- `--skip-rotation` flag lets `make verify-two-cluster`-style pre-demo checks run faster when the rotation window doesn't need re-proving every time.

- Full live run against real Podman-backed clusters took ~48s (with rotation); `--check-only` is near-instant; failure-path tests (bad `VAULT_ADDR`, bad `VSO_AUTH_ROLE`) were verified to fail at the correct section (4 and 5 respectively) with actionable diagnostics, and the baseline secret value (`larry`) was confirmed unchanged in both clusters afterward.

- Reused `scripts/check-vault-connectivity.sh` directly (via `bash "${SCRIPT_DIR}/check-vault-connectivity.sh"`) for section 4 instead of reimplementing the throwaway-curl-pod logic, keeping a single source of truth for that check.
blockers: full live end-to-end run verified against real two-cluster Podman-backed kind environment including rotation and reset
and all 8 test files in scripts/tests/ pass (91 total assertions across the suite).
